title: BCMA Antigen Escape Evidence Gap Register v0

gaps.gap_statement: The current public artifacts identify BCMA antigen loss, low density, and target alteration as mechanisms to track, but they do not estimate frequency across BCMA-directed products, disease states, or treatment sequences.
gaps.why_it_matters: Without denominators and product context, a public map can name a mechanism but cannot say how common it is or compare it across relapse settings.
public_next_actions: Add full-paper extraction fields for cohort denominator, product, prior BCMA exposure, target-assay timing, and relapse state.
Create a frequency-ready extraction checklist before any mechanism-rate claim is attempted.
what_would_improve_confidence: Multiple source-specific extractions with numerator, denominator, assay method, specimen context, and therapy sequence.
Expert review of whether source populations are comparable enough for any pooled summary.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that BCMA antigen escape is common or rare from the current public record.
Do not compare frequencies across therapies without aligned methods and populations.
limitations: This is a gap statement, not a frequency estimate.
The current public extraction uses public abstract and metadata context.

gaps.gap_statement: The claim set depends on target status, soluble BCMA, antigen density, and relapse definitions, but many public summaries do not expose enough assay, specimen, threshold, and time-point detail for stronger interpretation.
gaps.why_it_matters: A target-status claim can change meaning when the assay method, specimen source, sample adequacy, timing, or paired measurement context changes.
public_next_actions: Add required extraction fields for assay method, specimen source, threshold or scoring method, timing, paired measurements, and missing-context notes.
Update listing tools to flag claim records with measurement terms but missing measurement detail fields when those fields exist.
what_would_improve_confidence: Full-text extraction of measurement methods and source-specific assay limitations.
Expert review of which measurement fields are minimum viable for public comparison.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat a named measurement term as sufficient evidence by itself.
Do not compare target status or antigen density across sources without method context.
limitations: The glossary defines terms; it does not guarantee that source records contain enough measurement detail.

gaps.gap_statement: The SEMA4A-linked claim supports alternate-target research tracking, but the current public artifact does not establish clinical efficacy, patient safety, or trial suitability.
gaps.why_it_matters: Alternate-target hypotheses can look promising from antigen density and preclinical activity, but public artifacts must separate target biology from patient-level benefit and safety.
public_next_actions: Split alternate-target extraction fields into tumor target density, normal-tissue reactivity, model context, clinical translation status, and trial-registry status.
Add a target-specific evidence table only after source records support more than one SEMA4A-linked extraction.
what_would_improve_confidence: Additional translational sources, trial registry records, and expert review of safety-context fields.
Clear separation of preclinical activity from clinical outcome evidence.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that SEMA4A-directed CAR T therapy works in patients.
Do not recommend alternate-target interventions from this public artifact.
limitations: The current claim is explicitly preclinical and translational.
Clinical actionability requires clinical evidence and review outside this artifact.

gaps.gap_statement: The current claim set warns that BCMA resistance is multifactorial, but the non-antigen-loss buckets need more source-specific extractions before they can be compared.
gaps.why_it_matters: A cure-oriented relapse map should avoid over-weighting the mechanism with the most structured public extraction while under-extracting immune fitness, microenvironment, sequencing, and disease-context signals.
public_next_actions: Add source-specific extraction records for immune fitness, persistence, microenvironment, sequencing, and disease-burden context.
Create a balanced mechanism coverage report that counts extraction records and signals by mechanism bucket.
what_would_improve_confidence: Full-text extraction across multiple non-antigen-loss sources.
Mechanism-bucket coverage metrics before any prioritization claim.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that antigen escape is the dominant relapse mechanism because it currently has cleaner structured artifacts.
Do not rank mechanism buckets without balanced extraction coverage.
limitations: This gap identifies coverage imbalance in the public artifact set, not biological importance.

gaps.gap_statement: The current claim set and gap register are source-checked public organization artifacts, but they are not expert-reviewed educational summaries.
gaps.why_it_matters: Expert review is needed before these artifacts are used to teach, score evidence strength, or guide broader public interpretation.
public_next_actions: Use bcma-claim-set-expert-review-packet-v0 to collect claim-scope, measurement-assumption, source-quality, and safety-language review notes.
Track review status separately from source-check status and update target artifacts only after qualified reviewer comments are mapped to IDs.
what_would_improve_confidence: Review by clinicians, translational scientists, assay experts, and patient-facing science communicators.
Documented changes from review comments into structured artifacts.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat source-checked as expert-reviewed.
Do not turn these artifacts into patient-facing advice without a separate reviewed public education layer.
limitations: This gap cannot be closed by code alone; it requires qualified human review.

review_notes: Built from the BCMA antigen escape claim set, public extraction records, and measurement glossary.
Designed as a public work queue, not an evidence-strength score.
Issue #5 created a mapped expert-review packet; target artifacts remain source-checked until qualified review is completed.
